denial.reason_text: Member has not completed required conservative treatment. Physical therapy: 5 weeks completed, 6 required. No cervical epidural steroid injection on record. Member does not meet Section 2.2 requirements.

appeal_basis: Member has pacemaker — cervical epidural steroid injection is medically contraindicated
Physician documented interventional contraindication in chart
APL 22-014 myelopathy provisions apply — myelomalacia documented

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Progressive cervical myelopathy C4-C5 with myelomalacia

mri_1.findings: IMPORTANT: MRI performed with pacemaker-compatible protocol. C4-C5 severe central cord compression. T2 signal change (myelomalacia) confirmed. Progressive cord injury.

neurological_exam.findings: Bilateral lower extremity spasticity, hyperreflexia, impaired fine motor bilateral hands, Lhermitte sign positive

physical_therapy_summary.note: 5 weeks PT. Stopped due to pacemaker precautions limiting certain modalities and physician determination that further PT was not addressing neurological progression.

interventional.reason_not_performed: CONTRAINDICATED — Patient has permanent pacemaker. Fluoroscopy-guided cervical epidural steroid injection is contraindicated per anesthesiology consultation (Dr. Rivera, 2024-09-10). Written contraindication documented in chart.

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C4-C5 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — 5 weeks PT (need 6), no epidural injection completed.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: Does a documented medical contraindication to the interventional requirement waive that requirement under the plan's own criteria?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Does the pacemaker-related inability to complete the injection constitute functional plateau or documented contraindication?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: Does the myelomalacia finding trigger the regulatory override independently of the PT and interventional questions?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Can a plan deny based on a requirement that the member physically cannot meet due to a separate medical condition?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: The plan denied on two grounds: incomplete PT (5 of 6 weeks) and no injection. But: (1) the injection is medically contraindicated due to pacemaker — the plan's own criteria include a physician-documented contraindication waiver; (2) myelomalacia triggers the regulatory override regardless; (3) 5 weeks PT is close enough that the physician's documented determination of PT futility may constitute functional plateau. Multiple independent pathways to overturn, none applied by initial reviewer.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — Multiple independent grounds: (1) Interventional requirement waived by documented medical contraindication (pacemaker); (2) Myelomalacia triggers CIC 10169.5(a)(3) regulatory override making PT requirement unenforceable; (3) Physician-documented PT futility may constitute functional plateau. Plan criteria were applied mechanically without considering documented contraindications or regulatory provisions.
